612 Brhat Samhitd

but Sage Sarasvata, who is quoted by the commentator, gives it as
which Utpala takes to mean 4-^ cubits. If that be
correct, the reading ought to be instead of ^7^: which must
be a printer's devil. Hence I take the liberty of correcting the read-

If the ant-hill be to the east of the Pilu tree, there would be
water 4~ cubits to the south at a depth of 35 cubits. The signs would
be: in the first Purusa (5 cubits) a white and black snake of one
cubit length; to its south flows a vein with plenty of brackish water.
Should there be an ant-hill to the north of a Karrra (Garil)
